REMINDER: The MFU Letter alternates every other month with issues of the the MFU
Blueprint Reports - 6 times a year you get a new Blueprint Report, 6 times a year
you get a new Letter.
From: Todd Brown
West Palm Beach, Florida
Tuesday, September 4, 2013
Dear Marketing Funnel Superstar,
Like Dorothy, I’ve seen the wizard behind the curtain. And, just
like in the Wizard of Oz...
I Too Had Been Duped!
It turns out he was no wizard at all. But, just an ordinary old
man who travelled to Oz from Omaha long ago in a hot air balloon. And
he too was desperately longing to return home.
Only in my case, the Wizard wasn’t in a far off land. He was an
internet marketer. He was called a guru. And his story of rags to
riches and newfound life of luxury was intoxicating to me.
Even more: There wasn’t just one; there were many wizards. And I
drew motivation, inspiration, and excitement from each of their
stories and lifestyles. 	I looked to them as a benchmark for what I
wanted from my online business.
But one day, that all changed. It all came crumbling down.
That was the day I first saw the wizard behind the curtain.

-- 1 of 16 --

“Are you freaking serious, dude”, I said (with utter disgust,
disappointment, and a bit of nausea) as Brian Johnson shared things
with me for the first time, about many of the adored IM gurus.
Things that ninety-nine out of one-hundred online entrepreneurs
are completely kept in the dark about. Things you’ll hear about in
this month’s MFU Letter and future Letters.
I Remember It
Like It Was
Yesterday!
Brian was a partner and the Chief Operations Officer at
Strategic Profits. Rich Schefren, the founder, had just asked me to
come aboard also as a partner and something like the Chief Marketing
Officer.
I was still running two of my own companies outside of the
internet marketing niche. So I only came into the Strategic Profits
office once or twice a week to hold staff meetings and work on some
projects with Rich.
Brian, on the other hand, was knee-deep, five days a week,
working with high-end joint venture partners and fostering new and
on-going relationships with other well-known, top-tier marketers.
Usually, he was talking with the direct person in charge of
operations for these partners. The person, who, knew everything going
on in their companies. What was really going on. Not what was being
presented to the marketplace. But, the truth.
So, Brian heard it all.
And, saw it all.
And was privy to stuff almost nobody else knew about these
marketers and their businesses.
And, because of the popularity of Rich and the Strategic Profits
brand, he had this level of insight and access to almost every one of
the top direct response marketers online.
So, that day, when Brian first shared with me the stuff he did,
I had no doubt... it was accurate.
Sadly - or thankfully, depending on how you look at it - that
would only be the first of many times I would be given the explicit
and shocking details of...
-2-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 2 of 16 --

What Was Really Going-On
Behind The Scenes With
Many Of The Top Marketers!
With things like: their cash and lead flow. Refund rates. Profit
margins. Monthly expenses. Take-home incomes. Fancy homes and cars.
Even family relationships and “recreational activities”.
With guru after guru, story after story, and situation after
situation it was almost always the same - a lot of smoke and mirrors.
A lot of distortion. A lot of... what you see ain’t what you get.
Kind of like seeing Kim Kardashian for the first time without
all her make-up, without her photo-shoot clothing, without being
photoshopped, without the artificial lighting, and without the
strategically-placed camera angle and view.
Not quite what you were led to believe. Not quite what you
thought. In some instances, almost unrecognizable.
Now... look... before we go any further, I need to make sure
you’re crystal clear on something:
I’m in no way shape or form saying every well-known marketer or
“guru” in IM is a lying, cheating, stealing, S.O.B.
Not at all. Far, far from it.
There are a lot of decent, honest and ethical marketers doing
lots of good and helping a lot of people. Doing everything by the
book. And enjoying the well-deserved fruits of their labor. Many of
which, today, I’m grateful to call my friends. And a few who, I’m
sure, I’ll introduce you to in months to come.
But, there are also a lot of popular marketers in the IM
community... “household names”... that have presented an image and
story to the marketplace that is almost pure fiction.
In fact, over the two-years or so of running the marketing at
Strategic Profits, it never ceased to amaze me what I would come
face-to-face with when working with some of these top marketers.
Some Stuff That
Was Down-Right
APPALLING!
For instance:
-3-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 3 of 16 --

There were top marketers that would mess with affiliate cookies
to lessen the commissions they would have to pay affiliates and JV
partners.
There were marketers that either didn’t tell affiliates when
they made sales or just didn’t pay them commissions they had earned.
Or, didn’t pay them in a timely fashion... putting affiliates off as
long as possible.
There were marketers denying refunds despite their explicit
money-back guarantee. Or putting off refunds as long as possible...
until threatened from the customer with a chargeback.
There were marketers with massive FTC issues because they
included forced-continuity in their offers, but buried the notice of
it way, way down somewhere in their copy. If they even stated it at
all.
There were marketers quietly laying-off employees and shorting
them on their exit pay, as well as stiffing outsourcers money they
were due for projects already completed.
There were marketers with maxed-out credit cards, in default on
certain business bills, looking for a way to get a desperately needed
infusion of cash into their business bank account.
And I could go on and on.
The scary thing: These were all well-known, top marketers
recognized and lauded for their entrepreneurial prowess. With
customers and clients paying them to learn their “secret to success”.
Yet, in almost all cases, they were pulling these shenanigans
because...
They Simply Ran
Out Of Money!
That’s right. Many of these marketers... the same ones who had
previously conducted million and multi-million-dollar product
launches... now, had no more money in their businesses. They simply
ran out of cash.
How is that possible?
How could someone who, eight to ten months prior, had generated
over a million or more dollars in sales... now... have no money to
run their business?
-4-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 4 of 16 --

That, and more importantly, how to make sure it doesn’t happen
to you, is exactly what I’m going to lay-out for you in the rest of
this month’s Letter.
So, please, listen carefully. Read every word with intent. And
take each point I share with you with the utmost gravity.
Because, for the serious MFU subscriber, the rest of this Letter
contains truly priceless advice that will not only allow you to
completely bypass the same financial fallout I witnessed so many top
marketers succumb to... it will also, quite reasonably, guarantee you
have...
Life-long Success As A
Direct Response Marketer
And Entrepreneur!
Decades ago, Jay Abraham popularized a business-building method
that contributed to the engineering of many, many fortunes.
He presented this method as the ultimate business-building
shortcut. The fastest way to grow a company’s customer base. 	And a
way to financially leverage the goodwill, trust, and existing
relationship already established by a non-competing company.
He referred to these relationships as Host-Parasite. Later, as
joint ventures.
And, when used the way Jay originally presented them, joint
ventures were a powerful way of rapidly acquiring customers for a new
business and/or generating periodic surges and spikes of new
customers for an existing business. 	And they still are.
But, here’s the thing:
Over the years, the role of joint ventures has evolved,
expanded, and morphed. And, for masses of online marketers...
It Has Become Something
It Was Never Intended To Be,
Nor Should Be!
It has become the foundation of their marketing plan. The main
vehicle by which they acquire the bulk of their new customers.
Today, lots of online marketers - including the majority of
those top marketers who reached the end of their business cash flow -
-5-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 5 of 16 --

rely on joint ventures for the acquisition of the majority of their
new customers.
And it’s that reliance... and forcing joint ventures to become
essential and necessary for the acquisition of new customers...
Where Massive Business
And Cash Flow
Problems Begin!
Let me make a bold statement.
Possibly, one of the boldest marketing statements I’ve ever made
in writing.
Here it is: I don’t know, personally, of one single business
that has achieved any level of long-term sustainable financial
success, that has at any point relied on joint ventures or affiliates
to acquire new customers. (“Relied” being the key word)
There, I said it!
In fact, most (if not all) of the biggest direct response
entrepreneurs, businesses, and companies I know, that have sustained
over seven, eight, and nine figures a year for at least several
years, barely, if ever, utilize the strategy of joint ventures.
Talk to Martin Weiss of Weiss Financial... or... Mark Ford
(Michael Masterson) of Agora... or... Marty Edelston of Boardroom
Reports... or... Tom Phillips of Phillips Publishing... and you’d be
hard-pressed to find them discussing or proposing joint ventures as a
primary business growth strategy.
Ancillary, maybe. Primary, no.
Why?
Because they all know the catastrophic result of building a
business that is reliant and dependent on joint ventures for new
customer acquisition.
They all know the end result is exactly what those top
marketers... the ones who ran out of cash... came face to face with.
Plain and simple: They know that joint ventures are a fantastic
way to periodically enjoy a surge of new customers. But, that joint
ventures are NOT a consistent, long-term, reliable, or sustainable
-6-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 6 of 16 --

way of bringing new customers into a business. And, therefore, they
should NOT be relied on to do so.
Am I saying joint ventures are bad? That they should have no
place in your marketing plan?
Absolutely, positively not.
Joint ventures are great... when used correctly... as they were
intended to be used... as an extra, additional, periodic way to get
new customers above and beyond those you get from your reliable
front-end marketing platform (i.e. front-end marketing funnels).
You see, the problem is not in the strategy of joint
ventures. The problem is in the way the masses of online
entrepreneurs apply the strategy within their businesses.
And every time I’ve seen an entrepreneur misapply the strategy,
and build their online business solely off the back of joint ventures
and affiliates, the end result has always been the same. 	At some
point...
Their Cash Flow
Dries Up And They
Run Out Of Money!
Why does this happen?
Why do they run out of cash when reliant on joint ventures and
affiliates?
Well, for several reasons.
But, let me quickly give you just three.
First, the reality is... all joint venture partners are most
concerned about their own business, their own cash flow, and their
own interests.
Rightly so.
But, what that means is that if and when a better, newer, more
exciting promotional opportunity comes their way (as they always do),
they quickly ditch the current partner and are off promoting someone
else’s product.
Remember, what the best joint venture partners and affiliates
care about most is Earnings Per Click (EPC).
-7-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 7 of 16 --

And, the moment they can find a promotion elsewhere that brings
them a higher EPC...
Their Current Partner
Is Gonzo, Baby!
Second, joint ventures require the coordination of at least two
promotional calendars. And, when there’s any kind of a scheduling
conflict, the promotion often doesn’t happen.
Meaning: no new customers that promotional cycle from that joint
venture partner.
These type of conflicts (or, reasons for not promoting)
introduce lots of inconsistency in customer acquisition and front-end
cash flow. As well as lots of unknowns. All of which makes a business
vulnerable (and the average entrepreneur, stressed and paranoid).
Third, unless a joint venture partner is proficient, themselves,
at new customer acquisition, over time their list grows stale and
becomes less and less responsive.
Meaning: over time, promotions from those joint venture partners
produce less and less and less new customers.
Obviously, not good.
And those three reasons are just the beginning.
I could go on and on.
Like: Joint venture partners flake out. They go MIA. They wander
off into other ventures. They don’t do what they previously committed
to. They half-heartedly promote. And, blah, blah, blah.
Point is: The biggest, most sustainable direct-response-based
companies weren’t, and aren’t, built with a dependence on joint
ventures. And the big dogs of direct response marketing don’t think
of joint ventures as a foundational platform on which to build a
business or marketing plan.
And neither should you.
You want to do them every once in a while?
Fine.
-8-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 8 of 16 --

Every month?
Go right ahead.
Even every week?
Cool.
Just be sure the joint ventures you do are nothing more than the
icing on the cake for you in terms of new customer acquisition.
And, that, if they vanished tomorrow, you’d still have...
A Dependable Vehicle To
Acquire New Customers On A
Consistent, Reliable Basis!
Which begs the question - how do the most successful direct
response marketers design, create, and engineer these “dependable
vehicles”?
What do they do to go from start to profitable front-end
marketing funnel?
Well, there’s a lot of things. Far more than can be covered in
this Letter. But, let me share some of the biggies with you.
First, everything done stems from a recognition that the most
critical function of any business is the acquisition of new
customers.
And, that the sustainability of every business comes from the
front-end marketing that is responsible for the acquisition of those
new customers.
Hence: the front-end gets the most attention. It gets assigned
to the best, most talented staff. And it’s an area that is thought-
about, brainstormed, and tested more so than any other marketing area
within the business.
Now, just to be clear, this mindset is the antithesis of the way
the majority of internet marketers have been taught to approach their
business. Me included.
Most, have been taught that the backend of a business is where
all the profit is. And, therefore, it’s...
-9-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 9 of 16 --

Where The Majority Of
Your Attention, As A
Marketer, Should Go!
The truth is... yes... the profit does come from the backend of
your business. The front-end should be used to acquire maximum new
customers... most of the time at simply break-even. 	So, the profit
comes from all the rest of the transactions you generate with new
customers on the backend.
But, the long-term sustainability of any business comes from the
front-end. It comes from a marketing process or funnel that produces
a steady, consistent flow of new customers coming into your business.
Without the ability to consistently acquire new customers, every
business eventually goes belly up. And every entrepreneur eventually
runs out of money.
Just ask any of those top marketers I had to work with. Or any
of the...
Once Top Marketers... Now...
Forced To Work A Nine-To-Five
For Other “Upcoming” Marketers!
When you add to the importance of the front-end, the difficulty
of making an initial sale with a prospect, you should quickly
understand why the front-end demands your on-going focus and
attention. Much more so than the backend.
The backend is the easy part.
Assuming you’ve delivered the goods in your initial front-end
product/service experience with new customers, making the second sale
will be infinitely easier. And the third... infinitely easier than
the second. And so on.
It’s that first, front-end transaction that is most difficult.
It’s your front-end marketing funnel that requires the most potent
promise you can make. That requires your most irresistible offer.
That requires the greatest amount of proof. That...
Requires Your Biggest
And Best Ideas!
This is why the big dogs of direct response invest a significant
amount of time... every week... in thinking, brainstorming, and
researching. All in the quest of finding or coming up with a big,
-10-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 10 of 16 --

unique, fresh, and compelling idea to use as the foundation of their
next front-end marketing campaign/funnel.
Is it really that important?
You betcha it is! Because every wildly successful marketing
funnel... at its core... is built off of a big idea.
An idea that cuts through the clutter. An idea that wreaks of
newness. Is instantly recognizable as unique, different, and fresh.
Conveys a bold, audacious, almost unbelievable promise. Peeks
curiosity. Sparks excitement and interest. Titillates. Tantalizes.
And, in the process, completely prevents “mental opt-out” in the
form of, “Oh, I’ve seen that before”.
Of course, an idea like that is not always easy to come up with.
Or, quickly found. 	Hence, the need to invest ample time each and
every week in thinking, brainstorming, perusing, searching,
researching, and simply paying attention to the world around you.
This is...
One Of Those
Areas You Simply
Can Not Shortcut!
The majority of marketplaces have reached a level of “market
sophistication”, as Gene Schwartz would say, and no longer respond,
in large part, to big, hyped-up promises and claims.
Today, big ideas are the drivers behind the most powerful
headlines, and the highest-converting and most profitable marketing
funnels.
So, well before you put up any webpages, well before you map-out
your funnel stages, and well before you write one word of copy, you
need to take the time to come up with and choose the big idea you’re
going to run with for your marketing funnel.
Of course, over time the big idea for your funnel will evolve
and change based on response, timing, and prospect sentiment. But,
with a new funnel, you need to start with one big idea to get things
rolling.
And, when it comes to the work you put into designing and
engineering your front-end funnel, this is where you should invest...
almost... the greatest amount of mental and emotional effort.
-11-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 11 of 16 --

You’ll notice I said, “almost... the greatest amount of effort”.
That’s because despite being critical to your marketing funnel’s
overall success, the big idea is not the most important element of
your front-end marketing funnel. Let’s call it the second most
important element.
The first most important element of any and every front-end
marketing funnel is...
The Offer!
That’s right. The offer.
Second in importance overall in direct response marketing...
second to just the list of people you’re speaking with... is the
offer.
The offer is even more important than the copy - the words you
use to describe your product or service and its features and
benefits.
When it comes to your front-end marketing funnel, strategically
choosing the best, most compelling offer is paramount.
The right offer can radically impact conversions and return on
investment.
Yet, when it comes to the majority of marketing funnels online,
the offer is, almost, taken for granted. It’s an after-thought. It’s
simply a... “Hey, you can have my XYZ Widget. Just pay me X number of
dollars for it. And I’ll send it to you in XYZ number of days.”
You see, most marketers think the power of their copy carries
the bulk of the weight in how profitable their marketing funnel is.
They think the marketing message they create is what does the heavy
lifting in terms of conversions.
And, yes, the copy is important.
But, great copy will never make up for a crappy offer. Yet, a
killer offer can more than make up for crappy copy.
Did you get that?
No?
-12-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 12 of 16 --

Than let me say it again: Great copy will never make up for a
crappy offer. Yet, a killer offer can more than make up for crappy
copy.
To come up with a truly irresistible offer you need to invest
time. Time in research.
You need to research what type of offers your competitors are
making to your audience.
Are they mostly presenting hard offers? You know, “Pay first.
Then get your product.”
Or, are they mostly presenting soft offers? You know, “Pay
nothing today. Try the product for 7 days. And, then, only pay if you
love it.”
What type of money-back guarantee or risk-reversal offer are
they making? Thirty-day? Sixty-day? One-year? Conditional?
Unconditional?
What type and quantity of free bonuses (premiums) are they
offering?
What price-point are their offers at?
And what are they actually offering to new customers? What are
the deliverables?
Point is: To come up with a truly irresistible offer, you need
to first understand exactly what is currently being offered to your
prospects.
Then, once you do, your aim is to craft an offer so good... so
irresistible... so much of a no-brainer... that it...
Totally Crushes
Your Competitors’ Offers!
You want to craft an offer so far superior to anything anyone
else is offering your prospects. An offer so good it makes your
competitors’ offers look ridiculous. Like... why would a prospect
ever take advantage of their offer, when they have the chance to grab
yours.
Your offer should be so good it leaves competitors thinking
you’re crazy for making it. Wondering how you’re pulling it off.
-13-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 13 of 16 --

Your offer should be so good, prospects should almost feel like
they’re taking advantage of you. That they’re getting such an
incredible amount of value, at such an incredible price, with zero
risk.
And I’m dead-serious here.
Don’t underestimate the necessity of a home-run offer. It’s
crucial! It’s truly the bedrock of a high-converting front-end
marketing funnel. And that’s why I said...
It Deserves The
Greatest Amount Of
Attention From You!
Now, for the record, I believe one of the quickest and easiest
ways to begin crafting your irresistible offer is to start with a
“try before you buy” proposition. The kind I talked about in last
month’s MFU Blueprint Report.
I’m talking about your typical soft offer. Your trial offer.
Your “get it today and pay in 30 days” offer.
It’s the same exact type of offer that was widely and regularly
used by advertising legends like: Eugene Schwartz, Robert Collier,
Gary Halbert, Maxwell Sackheim, and so many more.
It’s highly-effective within a front-end funnel... arguably far
more effective than any other type of offer... because it eliminates
all risk for the prospect, puts the prospect in a position of control
and power, and allows the prospect to put all marketing claims and
promises to the test before spending a single penny.
But, that’s just the beginning.
Want to know how to really set this baby on fire?
Combine the “try before you buy” proposition with a lopsided
value proposition (i.e. tremendously valuable deliverables), high-
value product-enhancing premiums (i.e. free bonuses), a long time-
frame money-back guarantee (that kicks-in after they try it and
decide to buy), and an urgent reason to respond now (with reason why
given).
Use that type of offer within a front-end marketing funnel
that’s built on the foundation of a big idea... and boy oh boy... you
are on your way to having...
-14-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 14 of 16 --

A Serious Customer
Acquisition Machine!
Sadly, most internet marketers don’t have the balls to use that
type of offer. Most are afraid they’ll get refunds out the whazoo.
Get taken advantage of. And lose money.
I believe this stems from either one of two, or both, of the
following reasons:
Reason one: marketers are afraid to go with an offer like I
described because they honestly do not feel their product will
survive the test. They don’t feel their product is good enough. They
feel, given the chance, people will get the product, see it lacking,
and not want to pay for it.
For most, that may be true.
If that’s how you feel, let me tell you what to do. It’s simple.
Go back and make your product great. Make it good enough that
you would feel completely confident in putting it in a prospect’s
hands on a “trial” basis.
Reason two: marketers are afraid to go with an offer like I
described because they feel an excessive amount of prospects will
intentionally take advantage of them by getting their product, trying
it, and not keeping it. Or, keeping the product and not paying for
it.
Fact is: Yes, with a soft offer you’re going to get a certain
percentage of unscrupulous people who take advantage of you by
ordering your product, knowing full-well they’re not going to keep
it.
But, that’ll only be a small percentage. Most people aren’t
going to look to screw you. Most people are honest. Most.
Even so, the soft offer combined with all the other offer
elements I shared, will bring you a heck of a lot more new sales and
customers to far outweigh the percentage of people who chose not to
keep your product. So it’s well, well worth it.
Onward.
Armed with a big idea and superior, irresistible offer, your
first objective when assembling a new front-end marketing funnel is
to get to the point where you have a Minimum Viable Funnel (MVF).
-15-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 15 of 16 --

An MVF is a basic funnel that has just enough to be able to be
put to the test with paid traffic.
It most likely has a squeeze page. A sales vehicle (i.e. VSL or
long-form sales letter). And has a solid offer.
What an MVF doesn’t have is a built-out upsell/downsell
sequence. A fully-strategized follow-up sequence. Or, any advanced
conversion elements.
The objective at this stage? When you have an MVF?
To prove that the big idea behind the funnel resonates with the
market. And that the offer converts. Period.
Only after you've proven that the basic funnel converts, will
you then proceed to build-out the optimization elements of the
funnel.
Got it?
Well, I sure hope you do... cause... we’ve quickly come to end
of our time together this month.
I know, I’m bummed too. But, I’ll be back next month with an
off-the-hook (do people still say that) MFU Blueprint Report for you.
The funnel you’re going to see dissected next month is going to...
FREAKIN’ BLOW YOU AWAY!!
For now, my deepest hope is that you seriously take to heart the
warning shared in this Letter about building your business with a
reliance on joint ventures and affiliates.
Fact is: The only reliable, scalable, surefire way of growing a
sustainable business is by having at least one front-end marketing
funnel that allows you to break-even on the acquisition of new
customers from paid traffic.
And this month you got a peek into what happens to marketers...
even the biggest names in IM... who never develop that front-end
funnel.
And more importantly, you got a peek into the key elements used
by the big dogs in direct response marketing to create front-end
funnels that generate new customers like crazy! So, with that... I’m
out. Talk to you next month!
-16-
© Copyright 2013 - www.MarketingFunnelAutomation.com 	ALL RIGHTS RESERVED

-- 16 of 16 --
